summary: Matched ifStatus_802F6194 in src/melee/if/ifstatus.c by replacing a 99.81% near-match helper-based HSD_GObj traversal with direct control flow using explicit temporaries and goto labels. The PR states the result was checked locally in objdiff and in decomp.me.
what_changed: Removed the static inline helper nth_node, which walked an HSD_GObj linked list by n nodes and was part of the previous near-match implementation.
Removed the comment marking ifStatus_802F6194 as a 99.81% match with decomp.me scratch XGFpw.
Rewrote ifStatus_802F6194 to perform the traversal directly inside the function using gx_head, gx_next, gx_cur, and i temporaries.
Introduced explicit labels advance_node and check_done to shape the loop/control flow.
Kept the initial guard returning NULL when node is NULL or n is negative, then begins traversal from node->next_gx and advances via gx_cur->next until i >= n or the current GObj becomes NULL.
smart_moves: Abandoned a cleaner abstraction when the inline helper did not produce matching codegen; the final version mirrors the target control flow more directly.
Used explicit goto labels to express the assembly-like loop structure instead of relying on a for-loop/helper combination.
Split traversal state into head/current/next temporaries, which likely helped reproduce the original register and lifetime behavior.
Preserved null-safe advancement semantics by assigning gx_next = NULL when gx_cur is NULL instead of folding the logic into a higher-level loop.
Validated the match with objdiff locally and decomp.me according to the PR description.
decomp_lessons: A small inline helper can be enough to keep a function at a near-match; matching may require moving the helper logic back into the target function.
For linked-list traversal matches, explicit temporaries and labels can be more reliable than idiomatic C loops.
When matching compiler output, keep seemingly redundant or low-level control-flow structure if it improves binary equivalence.
Documented near-match comments should be removed once the function reaches a full match.
Use both local objdiff and decomp.me checks when claiming a function match.
naming_conventions: Preserved the address-style symbol name ifStatus_802F6194.
Used gx_head, gx_next, and gx_cur for HSD_GObj traversal state.
Used label names advance_node and check_done to describe low-level loop phases.
Kept the parameter names node and n for the starting GObj and index/count.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace helper-based traversal with direct in-function control flow.
Use goto labels to force the loop shape: initialize, jump to check_done, advance_node, then recheck.
Maintain separate current and next GObj variables to influence register allocation and instruction ordering.
Return gx_cur directly from both completion and NULL-termination paths.
Avoid over-idiomatizing linked-list loops when the assembly has a particular branch layout.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body/commentary states that the function should be a match and was checked in local objdiff and decomp.me.
follow_up_queries: Find other near-matches in ifstatus.c that use helper functions or idiomatic loops around HSD_GObj traversal.
Search for nth_node-like helpers introduced solely for matching and verify whether direct control flow produces better codegen.
Compare ifStatus_802F6194 against nearby functions such as ifStatus_802F61FC for repeated GObj traversal patterns.
Look for other decomp.me scratch comments marked below 100% that may be stale after matching work.
